div.h1: Bit Width vs Quantization Error Tradeoff

div.p: MSE (log scale) vs Bit Width — INT4 as practical lower bound

ol.li: Exponential relationship: Each additional bit halves the quantization error (doubles representable values)
Asymmetric advantage: 10-20% lower MSE for positive-only distributions (post-ReLU activations)
2-bit cliff: Only 4 values — catastrophic for any realistic model, unusable without extreme techniques
INT4 threshold: 16 values — requires GPTQ/AWQ for LLMs, works directly for some vision models
INT8 sweet spot: 256 values — negligible error (<0.01%) for most weights, standard for PTQ

div.p: CLIFF Only 4 values (-2 to 1) Catastrophic for most models
PRACTICAL LOWER BOUND 16 values (-8 to 7) Requires GPTQ for LLMs
SWEET SPOT 256 values Works for most layers
